rationale: Both regressions (agent_002, agent_011) share one root cause — the agent skipped running the project's canonical test suite before its first edit. In agent_002 it substituted an ad-hoc `python3 -c "..."` probe and treated the resulting `ZeroDivisionError` as a bug to fix (it was actually the intended contract). In agent_011 it edited at msg#10 before any test run.
change_description:
Added a prominent **⛔ GATING RULE (test before edit)** block at the very top of the Hard Rules section, before Rule 1. The block:
1. Makes the test-before-first-edit mandate unmissable (emoji + leading position).
2. Explicitly enumerates `python3 -c "..."` of ANY form as NOT counting — closing the loophole agent_002 exploited (the old Rule 2 only said `python3 -c "import X"`).
3. Includes the literal `ZeroDivisionError` on `divide(10,0)` example to inoculate against the exact agent_002 failure mode: a probe-raised exception may be the *intended* contract; only a failing test legitimizes a fix.
